correction.rationale: Use SAM point refinement rather than a polygon because the ankle-to-midfoot boundary and MTP line are curved and partially ambiguous. Positive points cover only the ankle, heel, midfoot, and proximal forefoot. Negative points suppress the rear foot/background and the distal toe region; the toe negatives are placed beyond the MTP line so the resulting mask stops before the visible toes.

judgments.evidence: Fail: the proposal is a whole-foot mask (about 12.8% versus the 0.2–4.0% contract), includes visible toes beyond the MTP boundary, and has background contamination and fragmented topology. Semantic side evidence supports right, so wrong_side is not assigned.

observations.contour: The contour is not anatomically clean: it includes the toes, has abrupt block-like steps and internal gaps, and appears to include background above and around the ankle.
observations.full_context: The visible target is the character's semantic right foot, consistent with the supplied COCO pose-chain evidence. The foot is planted in front while the other foot is partially visible behind it.
observations.mask: The mask substantially covers the whole foot including all toes, greatly exceeding the expected foot-base area. It also contains large holes and blocky regions that appear to be background around the ankle and upper foot.
observations.neighbor_overlap: The main contamination is background around the upper ankle/foot boundary; the visible rear foot is a neighboring part and should not be included, though the dominant mask error is the oversized whole-foot coverage.
observations.overlay: The red overlay extends over the distal toes and follows a broad whole-foot silhouette rather than stopping at the MTP line. Several rectangular and curved gaps expose inconsistent segmentation near the ankle.
observations.source_crop: The crop clearly shows one continuous bare foot and ankle, with toes distinctly visible at the distal end. The metatarsophalangeal split should exclude the toes from right_foot_base.
